content.problemStatement:
You are given two numbers NNN and MMM. A pair of arrays (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) is called beautiful if and only if:


‚ą£A‚ą£=‚ą£B‚ą£=N|A| = |B| = N‚ą£A‚ą£=‚ą£B‚ą£=N (i.e. both their lengths are equal to NNN);
For all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N, 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§M1 \le A_i \le M1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M and 1‚Č§Bi‚Č§M1 \le B_i \le M1‚Č§Bi‚Äč‚Č§M;
median(A)‚Č§median(B)median(A) \leq median(B)median(A)‚Č§median(B);
AAA is lexicographically smaller than BBB.
Find the number of **beautiful** pairs of arrays. Since this number can be huge, print it modulo MODMODMOD.

Note:


median(X)median(X)median(X) denotes the ‚Ćą‚ą£X‚ą£2‚ĆČth\lceil \frac{|X|}{2} \rceil ^{th}‚Ćą2‚ą£X‚ą£‚Äč‚ĆČth element after sorting the array XXX.
An array XXX is lexicographically smaller than YYY if and only if there exists some index iii such that Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi‚Äč<Yi‚Äč and Xj=YjX_j = Y_jXj‚Äč=Yj‚Äč for 1‚Č§j<i1 \le j \lt i1‚Č§j<i.

Input Format
The first and only line of input contains three integers NNN, MMM, MODMODMOD - the parameters defined in the statement.



Output Format
Output a single integer - the number of beautiful pairs modulo MODMODMOD.
